nativeRange.habitat: Riparian and marshland environments — typically grows emergent along the margins of streams, ponds, and rice paddies, but tolerates long periods fully submerged

decorPreferences: background planting area with vertical space for tall stems
best displayed in dense groups of multiple stems
nutrient-rich substrate preferred but tolerates plain gravel or sand with root tabs

avoidWith: plant-eating fish such as goldfish, silver dollars, or large herbivorous cichlids

imageCandidates.notes: Potted nursery photo from Modern Aquarium product page; shows submerged habit with narrow willow-like leaves on tall stems. Likely watermark-free product shot but copyright held by retailer.
Buce Plant product photo (1050x700) showing the submerged form. Retailer copyright.
Secondary Buce Plant product photo (700x700). Retailer copyright.

summary: Willow Hygro (also sold as Narrow-Leaf Hygro) is a fast-growing background stem plant from South and Southeast Asia with long, narrow, willow-like leaves. The plant sold under the name Hygrophila angustifolia is generally treated in the aquarium trade as a narrow-leaved cultivar of H. corymbosa rather than the true botanical species.
careNotes: Willow Hygro is a fast-growing stem plant widely considered one of the easier choices for beginners and low-tech tanks. Tall stems carry long, narrow leaves that give the plant its characteristic willowy appearance; it tends to perform best as a background plant arranged in dense groups of multiple stems, with frequent topping to encourage lateral branching. CO2 is not required — the plant grows well in non-injected setups — but supplemental CO2 noticeably accelerates growth and can produce denser, redder-tipped stems under strong light. Substrate is flexible: plain gravel or sand works adequately, though a nutrient-rich aquasoil or regular root tabs combined with liquid fertilizer generally give fuller growth. The plant tolerates a wide temperature range of 22–28°C, a pH of 6.0–7.5, and a broad range of water hardness, making it forgiving of typical community tank conditions. Useful for nutrient export — the fast-growing stems readily take up ammonium and nitrate, which can help suppress algae in well-planted tanks.
breedingNotes: New roots form quickly. Regular topping and replanting of cuttings maintains a dense, bushy stand and is the standard way of rejuvenating older stems whose bases have lost leaves.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Primary source. Buce Plant lists Hygrophila angustifolia as an easy beginner stem plant from Southeast Asia, family Acanthaceae, background placement, fast growth, moderate-to-high light, CO2 not required but recommended, pH 6-7.5, submerged height 6-12 in (15-30 cm), propagation by stem cuttings.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Cross-checked: 'Hygro Willow' / Hygrophila angustifolia listed as easy with moderate-to-high light, fast growth, midground-to-background placement, CO2 optional, temperature 72-82°F (22-28°C), origin Southeast Asia including India and Bangladesh, propagation by stem cuttings. Provided primary image candidate.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Acanthaceae, Lamiales) and the nomenclatural clarification: 'true Hygrophila angustifolia in the botanical sense does not belong to the group around H. corymbosa and is probably not even in cultivation' — the aquarium plant is a narrow-leaved cultivar of H. corymbosa.
